examples.title: Simple TCP Server
examples.description: Basic server that accepts client connections and sends response
examples.code:
SERVER PROGRAM
BEGIN
 CREATE socket at port 8080
 BIND socket to address
 LISTEN for connections (max 5)
 
 WHILE true DO
 ACCEPT new client connection
 RECEIVE request from client
 PROCESS request
 SEND response to client
 CLOSE client connection
 END WHILE
 CLOSE server socket
END
examples.explanation: Server creates a socket, binds to port 8080, listens for client connections, accepts requests, processes them, and sends responses back to clients.

examples.title: Simple TCP Client
examples.description: Client that connects to server and sends request
examples.code:
CLIENT PROGRAM
BEGIN
 CREATE socket
 CONNECT to server at IP address and port 8080
 
 CREATE HTTP GET request
 SEND request to server
 
 RECEIVE response from server
 DISPLAY response to user
 
 CLOSE socket
END
examples.explanation: Client creates a socket, connects to server, sends an HTTP request, receives the response, displays it, and closes the connection.

examples.title: HTTP Request Processing
examples.description: Server processing of HTTP GET request
examples.code:
HTTP GET /index.html

SERVER PROCESS REQUEST:
BEGIN
 PARSE request method (GET/POST)
 EXTRACT requested resource path
 
 IF method = GET THEN
 IF file exists THEN
 READ file content
 STATUS = 200 OK
 CONTENT-TYPE = "text/html"
 ELSE
 STATUS = 404 Not Found
 CONTENT = "Page not found"
 END IF
 END IF
 
 BUILD HTTP response with status header
 SEND response to client
END
examples.explanation: Server parses HTTP request, checks if requested resource exists, and returns appropriate status code (200 for success, 404 for not found) with content.

examples.title: Multi-threaded Server
examples.code:
MULTI-THREADED SERVER
BEGIN
 CREATE server socket on port 8080
 BIND and LISTEN
 
 WHILE true DO
 client_socket = ACCEPT connection
 
 CREATE new thread
 THREAD FUNCTION handle_client(client_socket):
 RECEIVE request
 PROCESS request
 SEND response
 CLOSE client_socket
 
 START thread with handle_client
 END WHILE
END
examples.explanation: Server creates a new thread for each client connection, allowing simultaneous handling of multiple client requests.
